summary: FQ-09C canon lock, art direction, and opening cinematic: the operator-approved canon bible, storyboard, background template, and work order are committed, and the opening shipped as an eight-scene ~42s 'Coheronia DOS Vector Cinematic' with a puppet acting layer. Authored 640x360 on a SubViewport at 2x nearest; a deterministic 10 Hz tick drives a pure (scene, tick) -> draw-command renderer (prologue_canvas.gd) with quantized plotting primitives and integer-zoom camera cuts; articulated keyframed figures (prologue_puppets.gd, 5-degree angle quantization) walk in, gesture, patrol and freeze, hammer with strike sparks, raise the roof beam, kneel to source the attunement pulse, and work the settlement. Engine-rendered text lock: COHERONIA / By Paul Peck / Where civilization pushes back. on the title card and persistent title screen; clean-profile autoplay, single-step advance, Escape skip that stops clock and audio, title-menu replay, profile-only prologue_seen; placeholder-safe audio cues; per-scene cel-shot hook (FQ-09V frame pools at 8 fps) for future hand-authored animation with the plotted rendering as permanent fallback. Two operator-rejected iterations (static panels, plotted-only) are in the history; suite 190 -> 203 (13 fq09c_* checks); haiku verifier NO FINDINGS.
scope: FQ-09C only, per docs/WORK_ORDER_FQ_09C_CANON_ART_PROLOGUE.md and the operator's mid-run cinematic rebuild directives - no FQ-09W/09A/09M work, no gameplay/save/balance changes, repo still ships zero art and zero audio.
next_action: FQ-09W: scene backdrops, underground darkness, and backing-wall foundation. FQ-09A prompt packs should include the opening's cel-frame sprite sheets.
